decision_description: The MAESTRO concept is confirmed aligned with topic HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02. Both expected outcomes are mapped at Confirmed status: EO-01 (improvements in AI agent autonomy, robustness, and reliability through advanced planning, memory management, and reasoning) is addressed by Pillar 1 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine with Monte Carlo Tree Search, hierarchical task networks, plan monitoring, and self-correction), Pillar 2 (Adaptive Memory Architecture with working, episodic, and semantic memory unified by a memory controller), and the cross-cutting External Tool and API Orchestration Layer; EO-02 (innovative multi-agent frameworks demonstrating effective decentralised coordination beyond individual agent capabilities) is addressed by Pillar 3 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination with formal task-delegation protocols, provable convergence guarantees, conflict resolution mechanisms, and auditable interaction traces). All ten mandatory scope requirements (SR-01 through SR-11) are covered. Constraints CC-01 through CC-07 are addressed at concept level; CC-08, CC-09, CC-10, CC-11, CC-12, and CC-13 are partially covered at concept stage, with full verification deferred to submission-stage geographic eligibility checks, control structure assessment, budget design, prior-project reuse enumeration, and synergy specification. No vocabulary gaps exist for EO-01 or EO-02. The scope_conflict_log is empty. Three concept claims are flagged for proposer attention in the scope check decision log entry (logistics demonstrator sector boundary, WP8 in-vehicle autonomous driving mapping analysis adequacy relative to CC-10 portfolio logic, and open-source SME framing lacking Tier 2B scope anchor) but none constitute out-of-scope determinations. Strategic differentiation is confirmed: integrated co-design of neuro-symbolic planning, adaptive memory, and decentralised multi-agent coordination unified by the External Tool and API Orchestration Layer, validated at TRL 5 across two call-identified Apply AI sectors (healthcare, advanced manufacturing) plus a cross-sector logistics transfer demonstrator.
tier_authority_applied: Tier 2B expected_outcomes.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_outcomes.json); Tier 2B scope_requirements.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json); Tier 2B call_constraints.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json); CLAUDE.md §7 Phase 2 gate condition
rationale: The concept_refinement_summary.json (docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase2_concept_refinement/concept_refinement_summary.json) records Confirmed status for both EO-01 and EO-02 topic mappings with empty vocabulary_gaps arrays, confirmed coverage for all mandatory scope requirements SR-01 through SR-11, and an empty scope_conflict_log — satisfying all four Phase 2 gate conditions stated in CLAUDE.md §7: topic mapping confirmed, call-specific vocabulary aligned with expected outcomes, strategic differentiation identified, and no unresolved scope conflicts. Partially covered constraints (CC-08, CC-09, CC-10, CC-11, CC-12, CC-13) reflect items that are structurally deferred to submission-stage verification rather than concept-level failures: geographic and control-structure eligibility of all eight consortium partners requires formal confirmation at submission; FSTP compliance is a budget-design-stage matter; prior-project reuse and synergy enumeration are proposal-strengthening items for Phases 3 through 8. The three flagged claims in the scope check decision log (concept_refiner scope_check entry, 2026-04-18T09:00:00Z) are advisory flags and do not constitute scope exclusions or unresolved conflicts under Tier 2B. The Phase 2 gate condition in CLAUDE.md §7 does not require resolution of partially covered submission-stage constraints before gate passage; it requires no unresolved scope conflicts, which the empty scope_conflict_log confirms.
